data.title: Baker Hughes: Award Features NovaLT Gas Turbine Technology to Be Provided to TURBINE-X Energy Inc. >BKR
Baker Hughes Secures Order to Provide Reliable and Efficient Power to U.S. Data Centers
data.description:
• Award features NovaLT™ gas turbine technology to be provided to TURBINE-X Energy Inc., part of Baker Hughes’ network of authorized data center packagers

HOUSTON and LONDON, March 13, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Baker Hughes , an energy technology company, announced Thursday an award from TURBINE-X Energy Inc. for its NovaLT™ gas turbine technology to address power demand growth from the data center market. This follows an announcement earlier this month that Baker Hughes will partner with Frontier Infrastructure to provide key carbon capture and storage (CCS) and power generation technologies for data centers and industrial customers.

Part of Baker Hughes established global network of strategic packagers, TURBINE-X Energy Inc. is a supplier of industrial gas turbine packages in combined cycle configurations for U.S. data center project developers and power producers. As part of the award, Baker Hughes is providing TURBINE-X Energy Inc. with its NovaLT™ gas turbine technology and associated equipment – including gears and power generation generator technology – for multiple data center projects across North America.

The NovaLT™ gas turbine is a multi-fuel solution that can start-up and run on different fuels including natural gas, various blends of natural gas and hydrogen, and 100% hydrogen, providing customers with the flexibility to adapt and meet their specific reliable and sustainable power requirements.

“Due to surging demand for generative AI, we see increasing opportunities for our power generation solutions to support behind-the-meter power requirements for data centers,” said Ganesh Ramaswamy, executive vice president of Industrial & Energy Technology at Baker Hughes. “We’re ready to drive progress at scale, leveraging our expertise and network of strategic partners to quickly provide market-ready reliable, flexible and efficient solutions to partners in the data center market, as well as broader industrial adjacencies where access to power is business critical.”

With a comprehensive portfolio of diverse, reliable and clean power solutions, including natural gas, geothermal, hydrogen, cogeneration, and the Net Power platform, Baker Hughes is able to provide market-ready, efficient and flexible solutions for data center hyperscalers, project developers, packagers and end users.

About Baker Hughes

Baker Hughes is an energy technology company that provides solutions to energy and industrial customers worldwide. Built on a century of experience and conducting business in over 120 countries, our innovative technologies and services are taking energy forward – making it safer, cleaner and more efficient for people and the planet. Visit us at bakerhughes.com.

For more information, please contact:

Media Relations

Chiara Toniato

+39 3463823419

chiara.toniato@bakerhughes.com 

Investor Relations

Chase Mulvehill

+1 346-297-2561

investor.relations@bakerhughes.com

data.title: Baker Hughes Secures Order to Provide Reliable and Efficient Power to U.S. Data Centers
Acelyrin Adopts Poison Pill as Tang Capital Partners Builds Stake
data.description:
By Denny Jacob

Acelyrin said its board approved adopting a limited-duration shareholders' rights plan in response to Tang Capital Partners' accumulation of a stake in the company.

The late-stage biopharma said the plan will issue one right for each share of common stock as of the close of business on March 24. The rights will initially trade with its common stock, and will become exercisable only if any person acquires 10% or more of Acelyrin's outstanding common stock.

Acelyrin said Tang Capital held 8.8% of its outstanding common stock.

Tang Capital didn't immediately respond to a request for comment issued through LinkedIn.

Poison pills, also called shareholder-rights plans, are legal maneuvers that make it hard for shareholders to build their stakes beyond a set point by triggering an option for others to buy more shares at a discount.

Acelyrin in February received an unsolicited indication of interest from Concentra Biosciences, a portfolio company under Tang Capital Management, for an acquisition at $3 a share.

Concentra's all-cash proposal, which includes a right to receive 80% of proceeds from any out-license or disposition of Acelyrin's development programs or intellectual property, came after Acelyrin agreed earlier in the month to merge with Alumis in an all-stock deal that is expected to close in the second quarter.

Write to Denny Jacob at denny.jacob@wsj.com

data.title: Lifetime Brands, Inc. Reports Fourth Quarter 2024 Financial Results
data.description:
Lifetime Brands, Inc., a leading global designer, developer, and marketer of a broad range of branded consumer products used in the home, has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2024. The company reported a strong performance in the fourth quarter, driven by increased sales and improved gross margins.

Financial Highlights

For the fourth quarter of 2024, Lifetime Brands reported consolidated net sales of $215.2 million, a 6% increase compared to $203.1 million in the same period of 2023. The company's gross margin also improved, reaching 37.7% compared to 36.4% in the previous year. Net income for the quarter was $8.9 million, or $0.41 per diluted share, up from $2.7 million, or $0.13 per diluted share, in the fourth quarter of 2023.

Business and Operational Highlights

Lifetime Brands saw a notable increase in U.S. sales, which rose by $10.8 million, or 5.8%, while international sales grew by $0.8 million, or 4.4% in constant currency. The company attributed this growth to the successful execution of its online sales strategy and new product introductions, including the Build·a·Board and the Dolly Parton program, which contributed $7 million in sales for 2024.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

In January 2025, Lifetime Brands launched Project Concord, a comprehensive turnaround initiative aimed at promoting growth and streamlining the cost structure of its international operations. Additionally, the company announced the relocation of its east coast distribution facility from Robbinsville, NJ, to Hagerstown, MD, which is expected to be operational by the second quarter of 2026. This move is anticipated to incur one-time exit costs of up to $7 million and capital expenditures of approximately $10 million.

Management's Perspective

Rob Kay, Lifetime’s Chief Executive Officer, commented on the company's performance, highlighting the strong fourth quarter results and the resilience of the core business. He emphasized the success of the online sales strategy and the positive impact of new product introductions. Kay also noted the company's proactive measures to mitigate exposure to tariffs and the strategic priorities of Project Concord to drive future growth.

Future Outlook

Lifetime Brands plans to provide detailed full-year 2025 guidance in conjunction with its first-quarter 2025 results in mid-May. The company will also host an Investor Day in the fourth quarter of 2025 to outline its long-term vision and priorities for operational segments supporting its global turnaround plan.

SEC Filing: LIFETIME BRANDS, INC [ LCUT ] - 8-K - Mar. 13, 2025

data.title: Ksolves India Board to Consider Third Interim Dividend for Fiscal 2025
data.description:
Ksolves India said its board of directors will meet on March 19 to consider the declaration of a third interim dividend for fiscal 2025 ending March 31, according to a filing with the Indian bourses on Thursday.

If approved, the record date for determining shareholder eligibility for the dividend will be March 25.

data.title: Capri says Donatella Versace steps down as brand's creative officer
data.description:
Capri Holdings said on Thursday Versace brand's chief creative officer, Donatella Versace, will step down and hand over the reins to Italian luxury brand Miu Miu's Dario Vitale.

The move comes amid reports earlier this month that Italy's Prada was moving closer to a deal to buy Versace from Capri after agreeing to a price of nearly 1.5 billion euros ($1.6 billion).

data.title: Trump Threatens 200% Tariffs on European Champagne, Wine, Alcohol — WSJ
data.description:
By Natalie Andrews

President Trump threatened to impose 200% tariffs on imports of champagne, wine and other alcohol from the European Union. His threat came one day after the EU said it planned 50% tariffs on imports of American whiskey, motorcycles and motorboats starting April 1, in retaliation for new U.S. levies.

"If this Tariff is not removed immediately, the U.S. will shortly place a 200% Tariff on all WINES, CHAMPAGNES, & ALCOHOLIC PRODUCTS COMING OUT OF FRANCE AND OTHER E.U. REPRESENTED COUNTRIES," Trump said on social media on Thursday. "This will be great for the Wine and Champagne businesses in the U.S."

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Mexico Industrial Production Contracts for 6th Month
data.description:
Mexico's industrial output contracted by 2.9% from the previous year in January of 2025, more than market expectations of a 1.8% decline, and extending the 2.7% drop in the previous month to mark the sharpest decline since March of last year.

It was the sixth consecutive contraction in industrial output, pressured by an aggressive decline in output of mined goods (-8.8%), including oil and gas (-10.7%) and metallic minerals (-0.4%).

Output was also lower for construction (.6.7%) and manufacturing (-0.8%) amid lower production of textiles (-5%), machinery and equipment (-6.%), and metal goods (-3.2%).

From the previous month, industrial output fell by 0.4% on a seasonally adjusted basis.

data.title: Janney Adjusts Price Target on Essex Property Trust to $312 From $308, Keeps Neutral Rating
data.description: Essex Property Trust has an average rating of hold and mean price target of $311.04, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Goldman Sachs double upgrades Schneider Electric on organic sales growth prospects
data.description:
** Goldman Sachs double upgrades French electrical equipment group Schneider Electric to "buy" from "sell"

** Brokerage expects organic sales growth to outperform the sector in the next two years

** It forecasts cyclical recovery in SCHN's Low Voltage construction (33% of sales) segment and continued growth in Medium Voltage and Datacenter segments(13% and 21% of sales, respectively)

** Goldman Sachs expects the company's return on invested capital (ROIC) to exceed the sector median this year for the first time in decades

** Expects Q1 results to be the next catalyst

** Out of 26 analysts, 15 rate the stock "strong buy" or "buy," ​ten rate "hold" and one "sell"

** Shares up 0.4% at 1148 GMT; dropped ~5% YTD

data.title: Spire Global Launches $40 Million Private Placement; Shares Drop
data.description:
Spire Global shares dropped 12% pre-bell Thursday after it said it signed a securities purchase agreement for a private placement with expected gross proceeds of approximately $40.0 million.

Under the terms of the deal, Spire said it is selling 5 million shares of its class A common stock at $8.00 each and $7.9999 per pre-funded warrant.

The company said the private placement is expected to close Friday.

data.title: LMS Capital Seeks Shareholders' Nod for Managed Realization of Assets
data.description:
LMS Capital plans to convene a general meeting to seek shareholders' approval to change its investment policy and pursue a managed realization of its assets.

The plan, which is also subject to approval by the UK Financial Conduct Authority, will result in the orderly realization of the investor's portfolio, according to a Thursday filing. No annual dividend will be paid during the managed realization.

In addition, LMS Capital is considering an initial capital distribution to shareholders.

data.title: Harsco Environmental: Located in Murga, Basque Country, Spain, New Plant Will Offer Environmental Asphalt Solutions
BMW Earnings Seen Lower, Guidance in Focus — Earnings Preview
data.description:
By Dominic Chopping

BMW is scheduled to report results for the full year on Friday. Here is what you need to know.

REVENUE FORECAST: Analysts expect the German luxury-car maker to report 2024 revenue of 147.71 billion euros ($160.83 billion), according to consensus figures compiled by the company and based on estimates by 13 analysts. The company reported revenue of 155.50 billion euros in 2023.

PROFIT FORECAST: BMW is expected to report earnings before interest and taxes of 12.18 billion euros, according to a company-compiled consensus. It reported EBIT of 18.48 billion euros for the year-earlier period. At BMW's closely watched automotive segment, analysts expect EBIT of 8.07 billion euros, which compares with 12.98 billion euros in 2023.

Shares in BMW have dropped 23% in the last 12 months to 84.06 euros.

WHAT TO WATCH

  • Analysts expect the EBIT margin for the automotive segment--a key performance indicator--to land at 6.4% in 2024 and 5.5% in the fourth quarter. The company has guided for a margin of between 6% and 7% in 2024.
  • BMW cut its 2024 guidance in September, citing costs of a recall to fix a braking-system problem and muted demand in China. The braking issue is largely behind, but the impact will still be evident in fourth quarter margins, HSBC analysts said in a note.
  • In terms of cash generation, Deutsche Bank's Tim Rokossa believes better fourth-quarter sales volumes had a positive impact on working capital and the bank looks for free cash flow of over 4 billion euros in the quarter.
  • The company's 2025 topline growth is expected to be primarily driven by the completion of the brake recall, with a larger improvement in volumes of higher-end vehicles driving a positive mix effect, Morningstar equity analyst Rella Suskin wrote. However, Suskin doesn't expect BMW to return to its 8% to 10% automotive profitability target range in 2025.
  • UBS expects BMW to guide for a 2025 auto EBIT margin of 6%-8%.
  • Morningstar expects BMW to provide some insights on the impact of U.S. import tariffs on the business. "Over the short term, BMW will incentivize customers looking at buying imported sedan models to switch to [its] locally assembled SUV models, as one strategy to reduce the impact of import tariffs."
  • In China, where the company generates around 30% of group earnings before tax, UBS expects BMW to proactively align its capacity and cost structure with likely structurally shrinking volumes.

Write to Dominic Chopping at dominic.chopping@wsj.com

data.title: Bosnia's Pobjeda Tesanj net profit falls 20% y/y in 2024
data.description:
SARAJEVO (Bosnia and Herzegovina), March 13 (SeeNews) - Bosnian hydraulic equipment manufacturer Pobjeda Tesanj [SAJ:PBJT] said its net profit dropped 19.5% year-on-year in 2024, to 946,266 marka ($525,704/483,818 euro).

The company's total revenue went down 14.3% to 23.3 million marka last year, while operating expenditures decreased by 13.9% to 22.3 million marka, Pobjeda said in its 2024 annual financial report on Wednesday.

Pobjeda increased its total assets to 35.9 million marka in 2024, from 31 million marka in 2023.

Pobjeda's shares traded at 31.06 marka by 12:30 CET on Thursday on the Sarajevo bourse, 0.19% up from the previous trading day.

Since the beginning of 2024, Pobjeda's shares have gained 51.5%, according to bourse data.

In January, Bosnia's Hifa Petrol and its owners, the Ahmetlic family, increased their stake in Pobjeda by 1.7% to 51.2% following a joint takeover bid.

(1 euro = 1.956 marka)
